description: The intrinsic, inalienable worth possessed by all beings, independent of status, ability, or achievement. It is not something to be earned or lost, but a fundamental quality to be recognized and honored in oneself and others.
rationale: The word opens with the reverent 'th', the breath of recognition we offer to what is sacred. The rounded 'o' creates wholeness, acknowledging the complete worth of each being. The grounding 'ma' roots dignity in the earth, in embodied presence—not abstract ideal but lived reality. Finally, 'nu' adds depth and purpose, the ancient understanding that every being carries meaning. This progression—from reverent recognition through wholeness and grounding to deep purpose—creates a sound of quiet gravitas. It cannot be spoken carelessly; the word itself demands the respect it names.
